title: Microsoft president Brad Smith has a message for students booing tech CEOs
content: Microsoft president Brad Smith has responded to graduating students who booed AI at commencement ceremonies this spring, calling the backlash a "powerful wake-up call for the tech sector." In a 3,000-word essay, Smith acknowledged the class of 2026 faces a "perfect storm" of AI automation and tech layoffs, yet urged graduates to adapt rather than fear AI. His optimistic message arrives as Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman, and Dario Amodei clash over AI's job impact.

title: 1,000 debit cards, Rs 95cr trail: US-based firm booked for funding conversion, Maoist activities
content: City police have registered a criminal case against US-based Christian missionary organization The Timothy Initiative and six individuals for allegedly funding over Rs 95 crore towards Naxalism and conversion activities. The ED complaint alleges the organization bypassed financial regulations using over 1,000 foreign debit cards, with funds routed to Left Wing Extremism-affected areas.

title: No handshake again! Harmanpreet, Fatima avoid customary greeting in T20 World Cup
content: The practice has been followed by Indian cricket teams since the Pahalgam terror attack and Operation Sindoor carried out by the Armed Forces thereafter last year. Indian players have avoided handshakes with Pakistan players at the toss and after matches since then.

title: Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
content: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: Why Elon Musk is the real-life Tony Stark
content: But Musk is more Tony Stark than Messrs Lee, Lieber, Heck and Kirby could have ever imagined. While Stark has his suits, JARVIS, and a defence-tech empire, Musk has Tesla, SpaceX, Starlink, xAI and Neuralink. If Stark could privatise world peace, Musk can swing a presidential election at will. Not to mention that Musk’s narcissism makes Stark look humble in comparison, as does Musk's wealth which makes Tony look lik

title: China's growing arsenal puts Australia in potential strike range: Report
content: China's growing missile arsenal, advanced submarines, and bombers now pose a direct strike threat to Australia, a new analysis reveals. Beijing's expanding capabilities, including intermediate-range ballistic missiles, could reach the Australian mainland from the South China Sea. This escalating military reach necessitates a deeper security debate for Australia.

title: Meta's highest paid employee Alexandr Wang 'admits' the company's previous AI policy didn't work
content: Meta's Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang has admitted that the company's open-source AI playbook no longer fits its frontier models, with Muse Spark kept proprietary after early training flagged bio risk and other safety concerns. Wang says rival AI labs are seeing the same risks scale up. Meanwhile, Meta is testing subscriptions on Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp and its AI chatbot to diversify beyond ads.

title: Quote of the day by Jackie Chan
content: Jackie Chan's quote, "Nothing makes me more determined to succeed than someone telling me something's impossible," reveals how he fuels his drive with doubt. The martial arts icon transformed criticism into motivation, breaking language barriers and achieving global stardom despite initial skepticism. His journey, from Hong Kong to Hollywood, inspires millions to persevere against all odds.

title: Virat Kohli and Karan Aujla’s Delhi collaboration sparks fan frenzy online
content: Cricket icon Virat Kohli and Punjabi music sensation Karan Aujla are set to create a stir with a joint event at Kohli's Delhi restaurant on June 21. The unexpected pairing has ignited social media, with fans buzzing about this unique crossover. Details remain scarce, but anticipation is high for this sports-music collaboration.

title: 8 dead after pick-up van falls into well in Maharashtra's Solapur
content: Tragedy struck Tandulwadi village as a pick-up truck carrying a family veered off the Mhaswad-Pandharpur Road, plunging into an uncovered well. Eight people, including four children and four women, lost their lives in the horrific accident. Villagers bravely rescued six survivors from the roadside well, which lacked any safety barriers.

title: Ayesha Curry shares family photos as Stephen Curry makes Hollywood confession
content: Ayesha Curry gave fans a rare glimpse into the Curry family's private life through a heartwarming Instagram carousel featuring Stephen Curry and their children. From sweet couple moments to proud family celebrations, the photos showcased the NBA star away from the spotlight. The post arrived just days after Stephen Curry opened up about how Ayesha helped him navigate his first Hollywood experience while working on GOAT. Here's a closer look at the family moments that captured fans' attention and the confession that highlighted Ayesha's influence beyond basketball

title: Pakistan players told to cover own travel & accommodation costs for Asian Games
content: The issue came to light during the ongoing two-day national trials in Lahore, where some players approached federation officials to ask about daily allowances and travel expenses for the event, PTI reported.

title: 'They start looking for your successor': Rohit Sharma faces blunt warning
content: Former England spinner Graeme Swann believes discussions around Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's futures are natural. He feels the upcoming England tour is crucial for Rohit. Swann is impressed by young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's talent. He also shared surprise at Suryakumar Yadav's removal as T20I captain. The focus remains on India's senior players and emerging stars.

title: Gujarat district tense after ‘idols’ spotted in 700-year-old ASI protected mosque
content: Bharuch police and administration have heightened security due to ASI concerns over a planned gathering of 5,000 people on June 15. A socio-religious group claims Hindu and Jain idols are within the protected Jama Masjid monument and seeks their preservation, fearing communal tension and damage to the structure.

title: NFL trade rumors: Patriots linked to $10M Giants pass rusher
content: The New England Patriots are being linked to New York Giants edge rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux in a proposed offseason trade. Bleacher Report suggested the Patriots could offer a conditional 2027 third-round pick for the former top-five draft pick. After acquiring A.J. Brown, New England may continue rebuilding aggressively under Mike Vrabel. Thibodeaux remains an intriguing option despite an injury-affected 2025 season, as the Patriots still need more pass-rush production heading into the 2026 campaign.

title: Dalit man beaten to death in UP's Lalitpur after refusing to massage feet
content: A 33-year-old Dalit man was allegedly beaten to death in Uttar Pradesh after refusing to massage the feet of three men during a drunken gathering. The accused reportedly dumped his body on a road to stage a road accident. Police have arrested the trio and registered a murder case under the SC/ST Act.

title: Somvati Amavasya June 2026: Do's and don'ts to follow on New Moon
content: Somvati Amavasya, a sacred day for ancestor worship, falls on June 15, 2026. Devotees observe fasts and perform pujas to forefathers. This year, engage in spiritual practices like worshipping Lord Shiva and the Peepal tree. Charity is encouraged to mitigate negative astrological effects. Avoid hair and nail cutting, applying oil, and consuming tamasic food.

title: Maharashtra man stabs women multiple times over personal dispute, chilling CCTV footage surfaces
content: A Palghar District Collector's Office employee, Snehal Sawant, was critically injured in a daylight stabbing near Gold Cinema. CCTV footage shows Anmol Mule allegedly attacking her multiple times with a sharp weapon. Police suspect a personal dispute, possibly relationship-related, as the motive. Mule is currently absconding, and a search is ongoing.

title: 'Innovation in India's DNA': PM Modi, Macron inaugurate 'Bharat Innovates' in Nice
content: Prime Minister Modi and French President Macron inaugurated Bharat Innovates 2026 in Nice, fostering India-France tech and innovation cooperation. The event connects Indian startups and institutions with global investors. Leaders emphasized shared vision and India's growing role as a global innovation leader, highlighting achievements like Chandrayaan-3.

title: Iran to immediately open Hormuz, US to lift Naval blockade: Draft deal details emerge
content: A draft memorandum between Iran and the US reportedly includes Iran's commitment to not produce nuclear weapons and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The US would lift its naval blockade and provide sanctions relief, including releasing frozen Iranian assets. Both sides are expected to finalize implementation details in the coming days.

title: Yogi’s UP beats national violent-crime decline across 5 key categories
content: NCRB data for 2022-24 shows UP outperformed the national trend in murder, rape, kidnapping, rioting and attempt to murder, though attempt to murder remains the state’s weakest decline

title: Maharashtra Home Department orders probe into social media content of Pranit More
content: The Maharashtra Home Department has ordered a probe into all videos of stand-up comedian Pranit More after his show sparked a major controversy. The Maharashtra Cyber Police registered an FIR against Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra and Sejal Pawar. Both Jangra and Pawar faced serious consequences, with Jangra losing his job and Pawar being put on compulsory leave.

title: NFL rumors: 49ers target $10M Giants edge before camp
content: The San Francisco 49ers are reportedly expected to pursue an edge rusher before training camp, with New York Giants defender Kayvon Thibodeaux emerging as a realistic trade target. NFL insiders believe San Francisco wants to strengthen its pass rush after struggling defensively last season. While stars like Maxx Crosby seem financially unrealistic, Thibodeaux’s rookie contract makes him a more manageable option. Other names, including Arizona Cardinals pass rusher Josh Sweat, have also surfaced in trade discussions.

title: Quote of the day by Morgan Freeman
content: Renowned actor Morgan Freeman recently delivered a thought-provoking message about the importance of living in the now, drawing inspiration from contemporaries like Jim Carrey. He highlighted the significance of finding peace in stillness, encouraging individuals to allow life to flow organically instead of relentlessly seeking to dominate every situation.

title: UP man stabs 19-year-old daughter to death inside police station over love affair
content: Tragedy struck in Banda district as a 19-year-old woman was allegedly stabbed to death by her father inside a police station. Recovered by police after eloping, she was reunited with her family at Badausa station. During counseling, her father reportedly attacked her with a sharp weapon. She later succumbed to her injuries.

title: Arhery: Dhiraj, Kumkum topple Olympic champions to win gold in World Cup
content: Facing one of the strongest teams in international archery, Dhiraj and Kumkum stayed in control throughout the match and made the most of key moments.

title: Indian vessel sinks off Oman; rescue op on to save 14 crew members as US navy joins efforts
content: An Indian-flagged vessel, Virat 1, carrying 14 crew members, has been involved in an incident off Oman's coast. Search and rescue operations are actively under way, coordinated with Omani authorities and nearby vessels. This event highlights ongoing concerns for Indian seafarers in the region, following recent incidents involving fatalities and diplomatic discussions.

title: Surbhi Jyoti welcomes baby girl with husband Sumit Suri
content: Television actress Surbhi Jyoti and husband Sumit Suri welcomed their first child, a baby girl, on June 13, 2026. The couple shared the joyous news on Instagram, expressing their love and gratitude. This follows Surbhi's pregnancy announcement in February and her wedding to businessman Sumit Suri in October 2024.

title: Another Sena split? Uddhav calls emergency meeting of MPs amid defection buzz
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) asserted all nine MPs attended Uddhav Thackeray's meeting, with five joining virtually due to valid reasons like family illness or election duties. This gathering aimed to quell speculation of a significant party split, following recent meetings between some UBT MPs and leaders of the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.

title: Sooryavanshi sends special message to Team India ahead of T20 WC clash vs Pakistan
content: Teen batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has thrown his support behind the Indian Women's T20 World Cup team ahead of their crucial match against Pakistan. The 15-year-old, who had a stellar IPL 2026, shared a heartfelt message on Instagram, boosting team morale. India, with a strong historical record against Pakistan, aims for a winning start in Birmingham.

title: 'Won't take body until encounter': Tension grips Dehradun after communal clash; what we know
content: Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami vowed "strictest possible action" against lawbreakers following a fatal clash in Dehradun. The violence, reportedly over a water dispute, left one dead and three injured. Dhami assured no one would escape justice, as police investigate and have begun demolishing an accused's property.

title: 'No disaster awaiting India': Sitharaman dismisses Rahul's 'economic tsunami' warnings
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman strongly defended the Modi government's achievements, countering Rahul Gandhi's criticisms. She highlighted India's robust economic growth amidst global challenges, citing IMF acknowledgments and significant poverty reduction. Sitharaman also detailed the government's welfare initiatives and infrastructure investments, particularly in Karnataka, urging collective effort towards a developed India by 2047.

title: Raushan Sir's brother, named in Khan Sir coaching vandalism case, dies in Nepal
content: The brother of coaching educator Raushan Anand, accused in the vandalism of Khan Sir's institute, has died under mysterious circumstances in Nepal. This development fuels speculation surrounding the high-profile case, which involves allegations of firing by security guards and a legal battle for Khan Sir's anticipatory bail.

title: ‘Will speak when time is right’: Saayoni Ghosh reaches Delhi amid TMC turmoil
content: TMC MP Saayoni Ghosh arrived in Delhi amid speculation of joining a rebel faction, declining to comment. This follows another TMC MP's arrival as a proposed meeting with the Lok Sabha Speaker looms. The party faces internal dissent, with a significant number of MPs and MLAs reportedly backing the dissident camp.

title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran Says No Point Talking To US After Israel Strikes Lebanon
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

title: In Trinamool Rebel Camp, 'New Entrant' Triggers Leadership Tussle
content: Sudip Bandyopadhyay has made up his mind to move to the rebel camp of Lok Sabha MPs, the sources said.

title: Video: UK Military Intercepts Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker In English Channel
content: Navy commandos boarded the ship under cover of darkness by fast-roping from a helicopter in the dark, according to footage released by the ministry.

title: Video: Israeli Air Force Strikes Hezbollah Headquarters In Beirut
content: The strike caused panic in the area, with people searching for survivors as smoke and dust rose from the building and its debris covered the street.

title: Amid Defections Buzz, Uddhav Thackeray Meets MPs, Sena Says All's Well
content: Senior leader and MP Sanjay Raut clarified that all nine of its Lok Sabha MPs participated in the meeting either physically, virtually or through phone calls, dismissing rumours of any internal rift

title: Delhi Police To Hold 'Thana Divas' For Public Grievances Every Saturday
content: The initiative aims to strengthen public outreach, improve grievance redressal and create a more citizen-friendly policing system through regular public hearings and greater involvement of senior police officers.

title: Who Is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran Guards Commander Driving Tehran's War Negotiations
content: Ahmad Vahidi serves as a deputy commander in the IRGC, one of the most powerful military organisations in Iran.

title: India Now Acts As Solution Contributor Rather Than Consumer: PM Modi
content: India has transformed from being a consumer of global solutions to a contributor of solutions for the world, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Sunday.

title: 14 Indians Evacuated After Vessel Suffers Engine Failure Off Oman Coast
content: Reports suggested that the crew members abandoned the vessel and embarked on a life raft dropped by a maritime patrol vessel.

title: "India Is Country Of Innovation:" France's Macron At 'Bharat Innovates' Event
content: French President Emmanuel Macron on Sunday said India is a nation of innovation, adding that both countries have a true partnership in critical sectors like AI and climate change.

title: Over 500 Indian Sailors Stranded In Strait Of Hormuz For 107 Days
content: Over 500 sailors have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman since the onset of hostilities between the US and Iran

title: Riots Bring Belfast To Standstill. How Race-Hate Tension Triggered Protests
content: During the violence, shops were forced to close, events were cancelled, and public transport was badly affected.

title: How India Walked The Middle East Tightrope Without Blinking
content: India's West Asia balancing act does not exist in isolation; it sits inside a larger geometry of partnerships.

title: Raushan Sir's Brother, Accused Of Khan Sir Coaching Vandalism, Dies In Nepal
content: Raushan Sir and Khan Sir had been at the centre of a major rivalry between two coaching centres in Patna, Gyan Bindu GS Academy and Khan Global Studies.

title: Automatic Weapons, 13 Bullets: Daring Chandigarh Murder Caught On Camera
content: The attack took place at around 2:30 pm. Three attackers arrived at the shop on a motorcycle; two entered.

title: Deadly Philippine Quake Raised Seabed By Up To Two Metres
content: A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as two metres (6.6 feet), exposing coral and harming...

title: 1 Killed, House Set On Fire As Water Dispute In Uttarakhand Turns Violent
content: Heavy police deployment was seen in the village as of Sunday even as scores of people gathered to protest against the incident and demand justice.

title: K. Muraleedharan refutes Opposition allegations on Nipah situation
content: He says there is no need for him to be present in Kozhikode as he could coordinate with senior Health officials sitting in Thiruvananthapuram as well

title: Congress to organise job fair on Rahul Gandhi’s birthday
content: Over 150 companies will take part in the Youth Congress initiative to be held in Delhi on June 18; unemployment, exam paper leaks have reached record levels during Modi’s tenure, party alleges

title: Telugus accord grand welcome to Naidu in Singapore
content: Busy schedule awaits the Chief Minister and his team during their two-day visit, which is aimed at attracting investments and forging collaborations

title: Kerala Assembly plans two-day orientation programme for MLAs this week
content: Over Wednesday and Thursday, a number of sessions will be held on the question hour, adjournment motion, resolutions and motions conducted under relevant rules, and parliamentary etiquette

title: CJP protest in Bengaluru LIVE: 'When we try to expose the broken system, we are called cockroaches,' Abhijeet Dipke says
content: Actor Prakash Raj says he has wish that Sonam Wangchuk should be Education Minister; The Cockroach Janta Party protest in Bengaluru is part of its nationwide campaign demanding the resignation of Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Four jump off train after fire rumour, crushed to death by another train in M.P.'s Morena
content: The four passengers who had alighted from the train failed to notice the approaching train and were run over, resulting in their deaths

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cyclonic system to bring rains to northern Tamil Nadu
content: According to the Regional Meteorological Centre in Chennai, moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorm and lightning is likely to occur in northern districts of Chennai

title: Suresh Kumar is new A.P. Health Secretary
content: Government transfers Saurabh Gaur and directs him to report to the General Administration Department for further posting

title: Bombay High Court seeks NIA’s reply on Varavara Rao’s plea to relocate to Hyderabad
content: The 85-year-old activist, who is out on medical bail, has argued that he cannot afford to live in Mumbai. The court has directed the NIA to file its reply within two weeks.

title: Indian-flagged dhow sinks off Oman; all 14 crew rescued
content: Virat 1 was sailing off the coast of Oman, reportedly with 14 Indian crew members. The incident comes amid heightened tensions in the Gulf region

title: Palaniswami criticises government for crimes involving TVK functionaries
content: He calls for transparency in the investigation of these cases and says there must not be any political interference

title: Post-mortem conducted on pet dog linked to search for missing toddler in Tuni
content: Blood samples and veterinary reports sent for forensic analysis as police widen search for two-year-old Sunkara Gnaneswari; 30 NDRF personnel join operation

title: Information Commission imposes penalty on two Social Welfare officials
content: Action taken for failing to furnish information sought under the Right to Information Act and for not complying with the commission’s orders

title: Mob disrupts Railway traffic near Patna, alleges lack of adequate trains for competitive exam; six arrested
content: Police fired eight rounds in the air, burst tear gas shells; top police offical says “more than 200 young men, posing as students appearing for competitive exams” turned up at the Pataliputra station

title: Amid ‘Operation Tiger’ buzz, Uddhav holds meeting of party’s Lok Sabha MPs; all nine lawmakers mark presence
content: After the meeting, party's lone Rajya Sabha member Sanjay Raut said all MPs were firmly with the party

title: Youth should join politics for nation- building, says A.P. BJP chief
content: Madhav says Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s goal of transforming India into a developed nation by 2047 can be achieved only with active participation of young people; urges Yuva Morcha activists to take the Centre’s development and welfare initiatives to the people

title: Vice-Chancellors’ participation at RSS event draws sharp criticism in Kerala
content: KUHS V-C Mohanan Kunnummal, MG University V-C in-charge Mavoothu D. and Thunchath Ezhuthachan Malayalam University V-C in-charge C.R. Prasad were present at the event held in Thiruvananthapuram on June 13

title: KVAFSU holds meeting with stakeholders to chart growth roadmap
content: Stakeholders, alumni and experts deliberate on strengthening research, infrastructure and outreach at the veterinary university

title: Modi, Macron inaugurate Innovation Conference in Nice, stress trust and cooperation in tech
content: PM Modi was warmly welcomed by the French President in Nice, where the two leaders greeted each other with a hug

title: Dredging in Korapuzha river to resume
content: The dredging work was halted following mass protests in February 2026. Residents and political parties had alleged that sand was being illegally extracted under the pretext of dredging

title: TMC crisis deepens, rebel MPs meet at Bhupender Yadav’s residence
content: The rebel MPs are also likely to hold a meeting with West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari on Sunday

title: Himanshu Jangra apologises for ‘₹370 biryani’ remark during comedian Pranit More’s show
content: Admitting that he “used some inappropriate words,” Mr. Jangra said that parts of the dating story he narrated as an audience member were "improvised" for entertainment purpose

title: Three test positive for drug use in Chittoor police crackdown
content: Police register a total of 15 cases as part of the cordon-and-search operation under the “Dhairya Sparsha – Operation Flush Out” initiative across the district

title: Rebel AIADMK leader C.Ve. Shanmugam accuses Edappadi Palaniswami of suppressing dissent
content: He said the party is falling into the clutches of a single family and accused Mr. Palaniswami of staging a “drama” to facilitate his son’s entry into the party. Mr. Shanmugam claimed that 31 of the 41 Assembly seats won by AIADMK in the 2026 Assembly elections were “alms” given by the PMK.

title: Kerala urged to invest in flu vaccination for the elderly
content: This year as of June 13, the State has reported 1,700 cases of influenza  and 19 deaths. Almost all of the deaths have occurred in the elderly. The elderly, especially those above 70 years, are disproportionately vulnerable to the flu virus and the complications

title: Shakthi teams to intensify campaign for safety of women, children in Sathya Sai district
content: Addressing the media, SP says initiatives organised as part of efforts to strengthen community awareness on issues affecting women and children

title: Shoolagiri wetland turns into toxic bed of industrial waste
content: The conscious dump of industrial waste in the wetland abutting a lake contaminates ground water and the water body.

title: ​YSRCP vows to stand by ‘DSC victims’
content: They have been deceived as the entire process is replete with leaks, system failures and corruption, alleges Sajjala Ramakrishna Reddy

title: Rebel TMC MP Kakoli’s son to return gift from Mamata, send legal notice over remarks
content: According to Kakoli’s son, Ms. Banerjee, during a press conference about a month ago, had referred to a TMC MP who was allegedly attempting to split the party after failing to secure a poll ticket for her son

title: Army drops colonial-era symbols, makes swords optional and introduces Bandi jacket
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics.
